ticket.title: Security review of auth token handling
ticket.description: Review how access/refresh tokens are stored, rotated, and scoped before the SAML launch. Confirm no secrets leak to logs.

candidate_memory.link_reason: the policy this review checks against

code_agent.rationale: Code sees the auth module, architecture, the SAML decision, and ticket notes; workspace policy and conventions are out of scope; both local notes are private — including Alice's own (an agent never reads a human's private note, NFR-E16-1).

qa_agent.rationale: Same in-scope codebase/decision/ticket set for QA; workspace entries are out of scope; the local notes are private.

design_agent.rationale: Design sees the workspace policy and conventions, the decision, and the ticket notes; codebase internals are out of scope; the local notes are private.

product_agent.rationale: Product sees the workspace policy/conventions, the decision, and ticket notes; codebase internals are out of scope; the local notes are private.

human_teammate.rationale: Alice sees every team-visible entry AND her own local todo; only the agent's private scratch is invisible to her. This is the baseline-owner asymmetry: her own local note is in, foreign local notes are out.
